fields.summary: Consents are not automatically sending to all new patients

content.text: Please see list of SF links where patient agreements were not sent to new patients.  This is a critical issue and needs escalation ASAP.  Our current “work around” is not compliant enough.  We are using emailed PDF forms to collect consent, but it is not a guarantee that we will receive it before treatment.  We are working on getting necessary team members licenses to Adobe Sign, but it’s taking time.
Not seeing the auto triggered email for the portal activation email and no consents were sent.
portal instructions were sent, nothing else was sent
noting has been sent to this patient

requestType.name: General Help Desk Request
requestType.description: Submit a Request or report and issue; fields below are based upon your selection.

content.text: to an appropriate Help Desk team member. They will reach out to you shortly with an update.
I’ve assigned this to our SF Admin to investigate. We will update you as soon as we have more information about this bug.

The engineering team was able to fix the issue with Informed Consents not being assigned for new patients, I have closed that ticket. However, the issue with Informed Consents being assigned to patients who have signed an Informed Consent within the last year is still occurring.

We’d like to change the logic so that new informed consents can be assigned for the calendar year they are created. For example, an Informed Consent 2026 should be able to be assigned in 2026 even if the patient signed an Informed Consent 2025 in December of 2025.

The use case is that new informed consents are created each year, and they make the old consents out of date, even if they were recently signed. To be compliant, we need to have the new consents for the year available to assign anytime between Jan 1-Dec 31  of that calendar year. Can you please scope this out and escalate to engineering if needed? This is a critical priority.

Here’s my proposed solution so it it no longer matches every agreement name that includes “Informed Consent.” It now requires the current calendar year in the name, an unexpired agreement (expiration on or after today). Still drops the PGT matches so an older year’s consent does not prevent assigning this year’s consent form.
#1 No Change: Name(Patient Agreement) CONTAINS ‘Informed Consent’
#2 New Condition Logic: Name(Patient Agreement) CONTAINS txt_CurrentYear
#4 Update Condition: Expiration_Date__c LESS THAN $Flow.CurrentDate >>> Expiration_Date__c GREATER THAN OR EQUAL $Flow.CurrentDate
Condition Logic: (1 AND 2 AND 4) AND NOT(3)
This looks good, please escalate this to engineering.
Open an Opportunity record that has a Patient Agreement named ‘Informed Consent_[CURRENT_YEAR]’
You will receive a Sent successfully prompt
